title: Dying Speech Of An Old Philosopher
content:
I strove with none, for none was worth my strife:
Nature I loved, and, next to Nature, Art:
I warm'd both hands before the fire of Life;
It sinks; and I am ready to depart.
meaning:
"Dying Speech Of An Old Philosopher" is a quintessential example of Walter Savage Landor's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
